DISCOVERY 612, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLTF'S FIRST SET OF INTERROGATORIES - NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BARBARA WILLS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. DISCOVERY OF THE LOCATION OF AN INDIVIDUAL WHO IS A POTENTIAL WITNESS IS NOT UNDULY PERSONAL OR OVERLY INTRUSIVE. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN FIVE DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED; ONLY ACTS BEGINNING IN 2007 ARE SUBJECT TO A TIMELY CLAIM AND THERE ARE NO TIMELY ADVERSE ACTIONS. IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF EITHER RACIAL DISCRIMINATION OR RETALIATION AND EVEN IF SHE HAD, DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN A LEGITIMATE BASIS FOR ITS CONDUCT AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT'S LEGITIMATE BASIS IS PRETEXTUAL. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Barbara Wills' Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Interrogatories is granted. Discovery of the location of an individual who is a potential witness is not unduly personal or overly intrusive. Further responses shall be made within five days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
